item.title: word_count
item.description: Vault Stats This vault currently contains 12,150 words across all notes.

item.title: Unlinked_Pages
item.description: Unlinked Notes Baseball choke Two grip choke on turtle Close guard overhook stiff Crusifix Straight ankle lock Grip break on upper back Cross collar grip break to takedown Collar grip break downward Double ankle grab pass Closed guard standing up Double under Closed guard figure 8 Toreando Leg drag ...

item.title: Empty_Pages
item.description: Empty Notes Hook Canto choke Kimura Estima lock Gogoplata North south Double leg Rubber guard .

item.title: Recently_Updated
item.description: Recently Updated Notes README Hook Unstructured notes Formatting index Kimura & Americana Three sisters Buggy choke Bow and arrow Windshield wiper choke .

item.title: Armbar
item.description: Generally useful and basic finisher which is based on extending the opponents are straight and pressuring the Elbow Fast and quick rules: Prevent rolling away, usually by having leg(s) over the opponents chest and face on different sides of the arm being locked in.

item.title: Unstructured notes
item.description: Leg attack sequence from no gi Reverse DLR Opponent counters with Estima Roll sideways to try to get underneath Leads to turtle or backtake, but needs to control the legs strictly If not possible keep rolling further and attack the other leg Remain on shoulders to better angle upward Original dlr le...

item.title: Figure-four grip
item.description: General Fundamentally a scenario where you are using two hands to attack opponents one.

item.title: X guards
item.description: Description Quite vague concept having the legs form a “X” shape while controlling the opponents leg(s).

item.title: Turtle
item.description: Lessons learned from attacker side: Pressure Turtle defence Variant of turtle is Panda On direction and hooks Always have the hook and rolling direction be opposite sides! Starting positions Rear Side Reverse (Head) Attack the neck In Gi start by attacking the neck with Cross collar -grip, in order ...
